reasoning: The Lean statement faithfully encodes the informal claim. "Vector space over infinite field F" is captured by `[Infinite F] [Field F] [AddCommGroup V] [Module F V]`. "Finite number of proper subspaces" is `[Finite ι]` with `u : ι → Submodule F V` and `hu : ∀ i, u i ≠ ⊤` (the standard meaning of proper: not equal to the full space). The conclusion `(⋃ i : ι, (u i : Set V)) ≠ ⊤` says the set-theoretic union equals `⊤ : Set V = Set.univ`, i.e., is not all of V. This exactly matches Mathlib's own formalization in `Mathlib/GroupTheory/CosetCover.lean` (`Subspace.exists_eq_top_of_iUnion_eq_univ`), whose docstring reads identically. No hypotheses are added or dropped relative to the informal statement.
